senses.definition: a notebook that is used for recording addresses, phone numbers, email addresses, and names of different people

examples.x: She keeps all her friends' contact details organized in her address book.
I lost my address book during the move, so I need to ask everyone for their contact information again.
The secretary updates the address book regularly to ensure all employees' information is current.
She flipped through her old address book, reminiscing about friends she hadn't spoken to in years.

senses.definition: a machine that answers missed calls and records the messages callers leave

examples.x: She checked her answering machine for messages after coming home.
He set up his answering machine to greet callers with a friendly message.
They listened to the messages on the answering machine from the weekend.
She was pleased to hear a message from an old friend on the answering machine.
They relied on the answering machine to capture important calls.

senses.definition: an enclosed place with a public phone that someone can pay to use

examples.x: She stepped into the phone booth to make a private call.
He found an old phone booth on the street and decided to use it.
They waited for their turn to use the phone booth at the train station.
The phone booth had a sign indicating the rates for calls.
She closed the door of the phone booth to avoid distractions.

senses.definition: the part of the phone held to the ear through which one can listen and speak

examples.x: She picked up the handset to answer the incoming call.
He adjusted the volume on the handset to hear better.
The cordless handset allowed her to move freely while talking.
She put the handset down after finishing the conversation.
He bought a wireless handset for convenience.

senses.definition: a direct phone line for emergency calls or calls between heads of governments

examples.x: Political campaigns use hotlines to encourage voter turnout and provide information on polling locations.
The IT department established a hotline for employees to report technical issues and request assistance.
Crisis hotlines operate 24/7 to ensure immediate assistance for those in need.
The suicide prevention hotline provides confidential support and counseling to individuals in crisis.

senses.definition: a phone connection using underground cables or wires on poles, rather than the satellite connection

examples.x: She still uses a landline for reliable communication at home.
He called his parents on their landline because cell service was poor.
They installed a new landline phone in the kitchen.
The landline provided a clear connection during the storm.

senses.definition: to enter a telephone number using a rotary or keypad on a telephone or mobile device in order to make a call

examples.x: She dialed her friend's number to invite them to the party.
In the past, people had to manually dial the numbers on rotary phones.
I'll dial the customer service hotline to inquire about my order.
He quickly dialed 911 when he witnessed an emergency.
I'll dial your number and let you know once I reach the venue.

senses.definition: to make phone calls to several people, particularly to receive information

examples.x: We spent the afternoon calling around to find the best travel deals.
She spent the morning calling around to inquire about job opportunities.
The team decided to call around to recruit new members.
I'm going to call around to see if any of my friends are available for dinner.
She called around to book a reservation for the anniversary dinner.

senses.definition: to return a phone call or contact someone again when the first attempt to communicate was missed or unsuccessful
to call someone on the phone
a person who is calling someone on the phone

examples.x: She recognized the caller's voice as soon as she answered.
The caller left a message after not getting an answer.
She asked the caller to repeat their name for confirmation.
He thanked the caller for providing helpful information.
The caller hung up quickly after realizing they had dialed the wrong number.

senses.definition: a person who manages telephone calls and connections, typically at a switchboard in a corporation or telephone exchange

examples.x: The operator connected the caller to the appropriate department.
Before automated systems, operators manually connected calls using a switchboard.
She called the hotel operator to request a wake-up call for the next morning.
The telephone company employs operators to handle customer service inquiries.
The operator efficiently routed incoming calls to different departments during peak hours.

senses.definition: a system that shows the phone number of an incoming call

examples.x: She checked her caller ID to see who was calling.
He ignored the call when the caller ID showed an unknown number.
He set up caller ID on his phone for better identification.
She was pleased to see her friend’s name on the caller ID.

senses.definition: to end a phone call while the other person is still on the line
to contact or telephone someone to have a conversation or communicate information

examples.x: I'll give you a call when I'm ready to discuss the project.

senses.definition: to remain on the line during a phone call, typically while waiting for someone to become available to talk

examples.x: Can you please hang on for a moment?
The receptionist asked the caller to hang on as she transferred the call to the appropriate department.
I'll put you on hold for a minute; please hang on until I return to the call.
Just hang on for a few seconds while I check that information for you.

senses.definition: to end a phone call by breaking the connection

examples.x: If the call quality is poor, it's better to hang up and try again for a clearer connection.
Don't forget to hang up the phone after our conversation.
He always forgets to hang up the phone, so the battery drains quickly.
It's impolite to hang up on someone without saying goodbye.

senses.definition: to wait on the phone line until the other person answers it

examples.x: Could you please hold for a moment while I transfer your call?

senses.definition: to connect a caller to the person to whom they want to speak
(of a person) free to be seen or talked to

examples.x: I tried calling my friend, but she wasn't available to take my call.
The customer service representative assured me that someone would be available to assist me shortly.
Is Mr. Smith available to take a call right now?
The professor's office hours are posted on the door, indicating when he is available for student consultations.
The receptionist informed me that the manager is not available at the moment.

senses.definition: (of a phone line) engaged in a call, meaning no new calls can be connected at that time

examples.x: She kept getting a busy signal when she tried to call her friend.
He found the line busy after multiple attempts to reach the office.
They heard a busy tone while trying to contact customer service.
They were frustrated by the busy lines on the day of the event.
She left a message because the phone was busy when she called.

senses.definition: a place with computers where people can pay to access Internet and often buy something to eat
websites and applications enabling users to share content and build communities on their smartphones, computers, etc.

examples.x: She spends hours on social media connecting with friends.
He used social media to promote his new business.
They shared photos and updates on social media during their trip.
He is concerned about privacy issues related to social media.
They discussed the impact of social media on society.

senses.definition: the opening page of a website that introduces it and links the user to other pages

examples.x: She visited the home page to find the latest updates.
The home page features a menu for easy navigation.
He designed the home page to be user-friendly and attractive.
The home page includes links to the blog and contact information.

senses.definition: the system in HTML in which data is being sent and received on World Wide Web

examples.x: They discussed how HTTP works in transferring data online.
The browser uses HTTP to fetch the website's content.
HTTP protocols determine how messages are formatted and transmitted.
She learned about HTTP while studying web development.
The developer checked the HTTP response status for errors.

senses.definition: a person who follows an account on social media
to add someone to the list of contacts on social media

examples.x: They will friend anyone who shares similar interests.
The platform allows you to friend people with similar interests.

senses.definition: a request sent through social media platforms or online networks, inviting someone to connect and add them as a friend or contact

examples.x: She received a friend request from a former classmate on Facebook.
After meeting at the conference, he sent her a friend request on LinkedIn.
He hesitated before accepting the friend request from his coworker on Instagram.
She ignored the friend request from the unfamiliar user on Twitter.
Sending a personalized message along with the friend request can increase the likelihood of it being accepted.

senses.definition: a file or document that is sent along with an email

examples.x: She sent an attachment with the report to her boss.
He opened the attachment to review the presentation slides.
They forgot to include the attachment in their message.
The attachment contained important information for the project.
She saved the attachment to her computer for later use.
They found the attachment to be corrupted and could not open it.

senses.definition: offensive and often threatening letters or emails usually sent under no name

examples.x: She received hate mail after speaking out on social media.
He found hate mail in his inbox, filled with negative comments.
They reported the hate mail to the authorities for safety.
The politician was targeted with hate mail during the campaign.
He decided to ignore the hate mail and focus on his supporters.

senses.definition: an online platform for communicating with people and making video calls

examples.x: We use Skype for weekly video conferences with our international team members.
The job interview was conducted via Skype due to the candidate's location.
He prefers using Skype to stay in touch with friends and family who live far away.
The company uses Skype for Business to facilitate remote collaboration and meetings among employees.

senses.definition: a real-time communication method on the Internet through text-based messages exchanged between users, typically on websites or through dedicated chat platforms

examples.x: Customer support is available 24/7 through the web chat feature on their website.
The students used web chat to collaborate on their group project remotely.
He engaged in a web chat with technical support to troubleshoot his computer issue.
The company implemented a web chat service to enhance online customer interaction.
During the webinar, participants can ask questions via web chat and receive answers from the speaker.

senses.definition: a form of online communication which enables the users to communicate very quickly in real-time

examples.x: They prefer to communicate through instant messaging rather than email for quick responses.
The team uses instant messaging to coordinate tasks and share updates throughout the day.
She sent him an instant messaging app to discuss their weekend plans.
She received an urgent message from her colleague via instant messaging about the change in meeting time.
Instant messaging is ideal for quick updates and urgent matters.

senses.definition: a person who spends a lot of time on the Internet

examples.x: The net surfer spent hours exploring different websites and online forums.
The net surfer enjoyed discovering new trends and gadgets online.
Many net surfers use social media to stay connected with friends and family.
As a seasoned net surfer, she could quickly find information on any topic.
The net surfer was intrigued by the variety of content available on the internet.
